To update your weight:

underweight.label: Underweight
underweight.message:
Your BMI is **{value}**, which falls in the underweight category.

**What this means for diabetes management:**
• May indicate need for more calorie-dense foods
• Important to maintain stable blood sugar
• Focus on nutrient-rich, healthy fats

**Recommended actions:**
• Eat regular, balanced meals
• Include healthy fats (avocado, nuts, fish)
• Don't skip meals
• Consider consulting your healthcare provider

Would you like meal recommendations to help you reach a healthier weight?

normal.label: Normal/Healthy
normal.message:
Your BMI is **{value}**, which is in the healthy range! 🎉

**What this means:**
• You're at a healthy weight for your height
• Lower risk of weight-related complications
• Focus on maintaining this through balanced nutrition

**Keep it up by:**
• Eating regular, balanced meals
• Staying active
• Monitoring blood sugar consistently
• Choosing low-GI foods

Your weight is supporting your diabetes management - great work!

overweight.label: Overweight
overweight.message:
Your BMI is **{value}**, which falls in the overweight category.

**Why this matters for diabetes:**
• Higher insulin resistance
• More challenging blood sugar control
• Increased risk of complications

**Good news - small changes help!**
• Even 5-10% weight loss improves insulin sensitivity
• Low-GI foods help with weight management
• Portion control is key

**I recommend:**
• Focus on fiber-rich vegetables
• Choose lean proteins
• Limit refined carbs (white rice, white bread)
• Stay active - even walking helps!

Want meal recommendations to support healthy weight loss?

obese.label: Obese
obese.message:
Your BMI is **{value}**, which is in the obese range.

**Important for your diabetes management:**
• Weight significantly impacts blood sugar control
• Working with healthcare providers is recommended
• Gradual, sustainable changes work best

**Steps you can take:**
• Focus on non-starchy vegetables
• Control portions carefully
• Choose low-GI foods consistently
• Consider meal timing strategies
• Stay as active as possible

**Remember:** Small, consistent changes lead to lasting results. I'm here to support your journey with appropriate meal recommendations.

Would you like to start with some diabetes-friendly meal suggestions?

noValue.default:
I can't calculate your BMI without your height and weight.

**What is BMI?**
Body Mass Index is a measure that uses height and weight to estimate body fat. It helps guide nutrition recommendations.

**BMI Categories:**
• Under 18.5: Underweight
• 18.5-24.9: Normal
• 25-29.9: Overweight
• 30+: Obese

👉 **Update your profile** with height and weight to see your personalized BMI insights!
noValue.partial: I have your {available} but need your {missing} to calculate BMI. Please update your profile for complete health insights.

explanation.response:
**BMI (Body Mass Index)** is a screening tool that uses your height and weight to estimate body fat.

**How it's calculated:**
BMI = weight (kg) ÷ height² (m²)

**Categories:**
• Under 18.5: Underweight
• 18.5-24.9: Normal weight
• 25-29.9: Overweight
• 30+: Obese

**For diabetes management:**
BMI helps determine:
• Calorie needs
• Weight management goals
• Risk assessment

**Note:** BMI is a screening tool, not a diagnostic measure. It doesn't account for muscle mass or body composition.

hasValue.default:

**How age affects my recommendations:**

Focus on:

**Why age matters:**

👉 **Add your age in Profile settings** for age-appropriate nutrition guidance!

type1.label: Type 1 Diabetes
type1.message:
Your profile indicates **Type 1 Diabetes**.

**What this means:**
Your body doesn't produce insulin, so you depend on external insulin.

**Nutrition considerations:**
• Carb counting is essential
• Consistent meal timing helps insulin planning
• Balance meals with insulin doses
• Monitor for hypoglycemia

**How I help:**
• Provide carb counts for meals
• Suggest balanced options
• Focus on low-GI when appropriate
• Consider your insulin timing

Want meal recommendations designed for Type 1 management?

type2.label: Type 2 Diabetes
type2.message:
Your profile indicates **Type 2 Diabetes**.

**What this means:**
Your body has insulin resistance - it produces insulin but doesn't use it effectively.

**Nutrition strategy:**
• Focus on low-GI foods
• Portion control is crucial
• Weight management helps
• Fiber-rich foods improve insulin sensitivity

**How I help:**
• Prioritize low-GI Nigerian foods
• Balance carbs, proteins, and fats
• Suggest appropriate portions
• Focus on blood sugar stability

Would you like diabetes-friendly meal suggestions?

prediabetes.label: Prediabetes
prediabetes.message:
Your profile indicates **Prediabetes**.

**Good news:**
Prediabetes can often be reversed with lifestyle changes!

**What to focus on:**
• Weight loss (if needed) - even 5-7% helps
• Low-GI food choices
• Regular physical activity
• Reduced sugar and refined carbs

**How I help:**
• Recommend foods that support blood sugar stability
• Focus on preventing progression to Type 2
• Encourage sustainable healthy habits

You're in a great position to take control. Want meal ideas to help reverse prediabetes?

gestational.label: Gestational Diabetes
gestational.message:
Your profile indicates **Gestational Diabetes**.

**Important notes:**
• This type develops during pregnancy
• Usually resolves after delivery
• Requires careful management for baby's health

**Nutrition focus:**
• Balanced, regular meals
• Controlled carbohydrates
• Adequate nutrition for pregnancy
• Blood sugar monitoring

⚠️ **Please work closely with your healthcare provider** for pregnancy-specific guidance. I can suggest general diabetes-friendly foods, but your doctor should guide your specific plan.

unknown.label: Not Specified
unknown.message:
Your diabetes type is not specified in your profile.

**Why this matters:**
Different diabetes types have different nutritional needs:

• **Type 1**: Focus on carb counting and insulin timing
• **Type 2**: Focus on low-GI, portions, and weight
• **Prediabetes**: Focus on prevention and reversal

👉 **Update your profile** with your diabetes type for more tailored recommendations!

noValue.default:
Your diabetes type isn't specified in your profile.

**Common types:**
• **Type 1**: Body doesn't produce insulin
• **Type 2**: Body has insulin resistance
• **Prediabetes**: Elevated blood sugar, not yet diabetic
• **Gestational**: Develops during pregnancy

Knowing your type helps me provide safer, more accurate recommendations.

👉 **Please update your profile** to get personalized guidance!

sedentary.label: Sedentary
sedentary.message:
Your activity level is set to **Sedentary** (little to no exercise).

**How this affects recommendations:**
• Lower calorie needs (~{calories} kcal/day)
• Smaller portions recommended
• Focus on nutrient-dense foods

**Tip:** Even small increases in activity help!
• 10-minute walks after meals can lower blood sugar by 20-30%
• Taking stairs instead of elevator
• Standing while working

I'll adjust my meal recommendations to match your activity level.

low.label: Low Activity
low.message:
Your activity level is set to **Low** (light exercise 1-2 days per week).

**Recommendations:**
• Moderate portions
• Balance carbs with activity timing
• Focus on sustained energy foods

**To increase activity:**
• Try 30-minute walks 3x per week
• Light stretching or yoga
• Household activities count too!

I factor this into your meal suggestions.

moderate.label: Moderate Activity
moderate.message:
Your activity level is set to **Moderate** (exercise 3-5 days per week).

**This is excellent for diabetes management!** 💪

**Benefits you're getting:**
• Improved insulin sensitivity
• Better blood sugar control
• Healthy weight maintenance

**Nutrition considerations:**
• Slightly larger portions okay
• Time carbs around exercise
• Focus on recovery nutrition

Keep up the good work! I'll recommend meals to fuel your active lifestyle.

high.label: High Activity
high.message:
Your activity level is set to **High** (frequent intense exercise).

**This significantly helps diabetes management!** 🏃‍♂️

**Considerations:**
• Higher calorie needs (~{calories} kcal/day)
• More carbs may be okay around exercise
• Focus on recovery and muscle maintenance
• Watch for exercise-induced hypoglycemia

**Nutrition focus:**
• Pre-workout fuel (1-2 hours before)
• Post-workout recovery (within 30 mins)
• Adequate protein
• Hydration

I'll tailor recommendations to support your active lifestyle!

noValue.default:
Your activity level isn't specified in your profile.

**Activity levels:**
• **Sedentary**: Desk job, little exercise
• **Low**: Light exercise 1-2 days/week
• **Moderate**: Exercise 3-5 days/week
• **High**: Daily intense exercise

**Why it matters:**
• Determines calorie needs
• Affects carb recommendations
• Influences portion sizes

👉 **Update your profile** for personalized recommendations!

exerciseTips.response:
**Exercise & Diabetes Management** 🏃‍♂️

**Best activities for blood sugar control:**
• Brisk walking (30 min/day) - reduces blood sugar by 20-30%
• Swimming - low impact, full body
• Cycling - great for joints
• Strength training 2-3x/week - builds insulin-sensitive muscle

**Benefits:**
• Lowers blood sugar for up to 24 hours
• Improves insulin sensitivity
• Helps weight management
• Reduces stress

**Safety tips:**
• Check blood sugar before exercise
• Carry fast-acting glucose (pure honey, glucose tablets)
• Stay hydrated
• Exercise 1-3 hours after meals
• Start slow, progress gradually

Would you like meal suggestions to fuel your workouts?

Your recorded food allergies/intolerances are:

{list}

**How I handle this:**
✅ I automatically exclude these foods from recommendations

**Your safety is my priority!** All meal recommendations will avoid these items.

{alternatives}

hasValue.singleAllergy:
You've recorded an allergy/intolerance to **{item}**.

I'll make sure to exclude this from all my meal recommendations and suggest safe alternatives that still meet your nutritional needs.

noValue.default:
You haven't recorded any food allergies or intolerances.

**Common allergens to consider:**
• 🥜 Groundnuts (peanuts)/tree nuts
• 🦐 Seafood/shellfish (crayfish, prawns, fish)
• 🥛 Dairy/lactose
• 🌾 Gluten/wheat
• 🥚 Eggs
• 🫘 Soy

**If you have allergies:**
👉 Add them to your profile for safer recommendations!

This ensures I never suggest foods that could harm you.

commonAllergies.response:
**Common Food Allergies:**

🥜 **Groundnuts/Tree Nuts** - Can cause severe reactions
🦐 **Seafood/Shellfish** - Includes crayfish, prawns, fish
🥛 **Dairy/Lactose** - Milk, cheese, yogurt, peak milk
🌾 **Gluten/Wheat** - Found in bread, semovita, wheat-based swallows
🥚 **Eggs** - Including products containing eggs
🫘 **Soy** - Found in many processed foods

**Nigerian-specific considerations:**
• Crayfish (shellfish allergy)
• Palm oil (tree nut related)
• Ogiri/dawadawa (soy-based)

**If you have any of these:**
Add them to your profile so I can provide safe meal recommendations!

low.label: Budget-Friendly
low.message:
Your budget preference is set to **Budget-Friendly**.

**How I help:**
• Prioritize affordable, nutritious options
• Suggest locally available ingredients
• Focus on cost-effective proteins (beans, eggs)
• Recommend seasonal produce

**Budget-friendly diabetes tips:**
• Beans and legumes (₦200-500) are low-GI and filling
• Eggs (₦100-200) are versatile and nutritious
• Vegetables like ewedu, ugwu, okra are affordable
• Ofada rice is cheaper than imported rice

I'll make sure recommendations fit your budget!

middle.label: Moderate Budget
middle.message:
Your budget preference is set to **Moderate**.

**This gives good flexibility!**
• Mix of affordable and quality ingredients
• Occasional premium options
• Good variety in meal choices

**I'll recommend:**
• Balanced options with good value
• Mix of proteins (fish, chicken, beans)
• Fresh produce and quality staples
• Some specialty items when beneficial

You have a good range of diabetes-friendly options!

high.label: Premium Budget
high.message:
Your budget preference is set to **Premium**.

**More options available:**
• Wider variety of proteins
• Fresh, quality ingredients
• Specialty items when beneficial
• Diverse meal options

**I can recommend:**
• Premium fish (tilapia, mackerel, salmon)
• Quality meats (turkey, lean beef)
• Organic options when available
• Imported whole grains
• Greater variety in meals

You have the most flexibility in meal choices!

noValue.default:
Your budget preference isn't set.

**Budget options:**
• **Low**: Focus on affordable, nutritious basics (beans, eggs, local vegetables)
• **Middle**: Balanced mix of value and quality (fish, chicken, variety)
• **High**: More flexibility and variety (premium proteins, specialty items)

**Why it matters:**
I'll recommend meals that fit your financial situation without compromising on nutrition.

👉 **Update your profile** for budget-appropriate suggestions!

budgetTips.response:
**Eating Healthy on a Budget** 💰

**Affordable diabetes-friendly Nigerian foods:**
• 🫘 Beans (gbegiri, ewa) - ₦200-400/cup, low-GI, high protein
• 🥚 Eggs - ₦100-200 each, versatile, nutritious
• 🥬 Local vegetables (ewedu, ugwu, waterleaf) - ₦50-200
• 🍚 Ofada/Abakaliki rice - cheaper than imported, lower GI
• 🐟 Titus fish (mackerel) - affordable protein

**Money-saving tips:**
• Buy from local markets (Mile 12, Oyingbo, etc.)
• Cook meals at home
• Plan weekly meals to reduce waste
• Buy seasonal vegetables
• Avoid processed foods (often expensive AND unhealthy)

Healthy diabetes eating doesn't have to be expensive!

morning.template:
Based on our conversation, you had **{morningMeal}** for breakfast.

For your next meal, I'll suggest options that:
• Complement your morning nutrition
• Maintain balanced daily intake
• Avoid repetition
• Keep your blood sugar stable

Would you like lunch suggestions?
morning.followUp: Since you had {morningMeal} this morning (which provided {nutrients}), for lunch I recommend something that balances your day with {complementaryNutrients}.

afternoon.template:
Today so far:
• **Breakfast**: {morningMeal}

For lunch, I'll recommend meals that:
• Don't repeat what you had
• Balance your protein and carbs for the day
• Keep blood sugar steady through the afternoon

What type of lunch are you in the mood for?
afternoon.followUp: Considering your breakfast, here are complementary lunch options that will fuel your afternoon without spiking blood sugar.

evening.template:
Today's meals so far:
• **Breakfast**: {morningMeal}
• **Lunch**: {afternoonMeal}

For dinner, I'll suggest meals that:
• Complete your daily nutrition
• Are lighter (since it's evening)
• Won't spike blood sugar before bed
• Provide any nutrients you might have missed today
evening.followUp: Based on your meals today, dinner should be lighter and lower-carb to maintain good overnight blood sugar levels.

mealHistory.noHistory:
I don't have a record of your previous meals in this conversation.

Want me to recommend a meal? Just tap the 🍽️ button and select your meal type, or tell me what you're looking for!

followUpMeal.transition: For {currentMealType}, I'll suggest options that complement what you've already eaten.
followUpMeal.nutritionBalance:
Your {previousMealType} provided: {previousNutrients}

To balance your day, your {currentMealType} should focus on: {neededNutrients}

repeatRequest.response:
You enjoyed **{previousMeal}** - I can suggest:

1. **Exact repeat** - The same meal again
2. **Similar options** - Same nutritional profile, different ingredients
3. **Variation** - Same base, different preparation

What sounds good to you?

mealProgression.description: Track meal progression throughout the day

rules.guidance: Lunch should be slightly larger than breakfast, with balanced protein and moderate carbs
Dinner should be lighter than lunch, lower in carbs for better overnight blood sugar
Snacks should be small, protein-rich or fiber-rich, low-carb to prevent spikes

conversationMemory.description: How to use conversation context for better recommendations

previousMeals.use: Track foods mentioned to avoid repetition and ensure variety
previousMeals.example: If user asked about jollof rice for breakfast, suggest something different for lunch

preferences.example: If user says they don't like beans, avoid bean recommendations

concerns.example: If user mentioned blood sugar spikes, prioritize very low-GI options

goals.example: If user wants to lose weight, suggest smaller portions and more vegetables

balancing.carbs: If breakfast was carb-heavy, suggest protein-focused lunch
balancing.protein: If lunch was protein-heavy, dinner can have moderate carbs
balancing.vegetables: If previous meals lacked vegetables, emphasize them in next meal
balancing.fiber: Track fiber intake and suggest fiber-rich options if lacking
